channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Loose kerb stone of Anerley Road, near junction of Anerley Vale, 10m south of bus stop, 2nd March
item.description:
Kerb of Anerley Road, near junction of Anerley Vale, 10m south of bus stop, is loose and wobbly

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Anerley Vale, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: "Landsdown Place" Road sign damaged/faded, on the junction of Belvedere Road, 2nd March
item.description:
The road sign for Landsdown Place, on the north end of this road, is faded/damaged. Could it be repaired/replaced, please?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 3, Lansdowne Place, Upper Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: One way arrow sign's light is out, on junction of Lansdown Place with Belvedere Road, 2nd March
item.description:
Light has a sticker depicting ID L775 on column SSL 6

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 3, Lansdowne Place, Upper Norwood, Greater London

item.description:
Column number P401

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cintra Park, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Detritus & litter on padestrianised area, 2nd March
item.description:
The pedestrianised area in front of 72 Anerley Road has a lot of muck and litter, could it be tidied up, please?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 72-78, Anerley Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tesco's delviery cages stored and blocking pavement, 2nd March
item.description:
Delivery cages on the public pavement, for general storage of the cages themselves and to contain waste disposal, leaves an unsightly mess and also blocks the pavement in a high footfall area. Reducing sightlines for public safety, and also reducing the ease of access for those with accessibility needs. I believe these cages should not be on the public pavement, but should instead be placed inside your shop storage areas, or kept in the area to the rear of your shop for both storage, and waste disposal. This issue is blocking the pavement, a public place, and so should be enforced by the council, please? If I have filed this under the wrong category here on FMS, which category should I redirect it to, please?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 72-78, Anerley Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Giant dog poo, 2nd March
item.description:
Between 1 Tyrrell Road and Barry Road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Tyrrell Road, East Dulwich, Greater London

item.title: Giant dog poo, 2nd March
item.description:
Between 1 Tyrrell Road and Barry Road

item.title: Injured fox laying on pavement that has since passed away, 2nd March
item.description:
There was a injured fox on the pavement outside my house which has now passed away.i have double bagged it up and put it in a container until collection.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Fairfield Grove, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lots of litter to rear of Miller House, 2nd March
item.description:
There is alot of litter and detritus to the rear of Miller House on Anerley Vale. This should be cleared up, or if the land is not of the council's responsibility, enforcement action should be taken on those responsible of the land as repated littering can be seen here.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Anerley Vale, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned bike, 2nd March
item.description:
Lime bike corner of Tiverton road / sparrows lane se9 . Obstucting road . Please pick up

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 74, Sparrows Lane, New Eltham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree causing ongoing damage, 2nd March
item.description:
The verminous tree outside our house continues to cause problems to us and our neighbours. Damage to property has been reported on numerous occasions before without any corrective action being taken to remedy. It’s huge ugly size is ruining the pavement, our front wall, drainage and outlook.Now the ghastly annual sap is ruining the paintwork of ours and neighbours cars now needing professional cleaning, it is over our house windows and sills which require repeated chemical cleaning . We are all sick to death of repeatedly reporting the damage and it causes . Soon the winter foliage will fall blocking gutters and drainage- nothing is ever done other than bodged repairs to the dangerous pavement and clearing the blockage to road gutters, it will soon be twice the size of our house- clearly a totally inappropriate choice of tree and should be removed immediately.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kingsway, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pile of flytipped doors/wood on pavement, 2nd March
item.description:
Pile of flytipped doors/wood on pavement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rectory Lane, Albany Park, Greater London

item.title: Diversion signs narrowing the pavement, 2nd March
item.description:
Again in Heath Way, the road diversion signs have been placed which restricts the pavement for users, wheelchair users, pram users. On a school run.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Heathway, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
